# grepr_pipeline (Resource)

Manages a Grepr pipeline (async streaming job).

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    grepr = {
      source = "grepr/grepr"
    }
  }
}

provider "grepr" {
  # Configure via environment variables:
  # GREPR_HOST, GREPR_CLIENT_ID, GREPR_CLIENT_SECRET
}

resource "grepr_pipeline" "example" {
  name = "example_pipeline"

  job_graph_json = file("${path.module}/pipeline.json")

  desired_state = "RUNNING"

  tags = {
    environment = "example"
    managed_by  = "terraform"
  }

  wait_for_state = true
  state_timeout  = 300
}

output "pipeline_id" {
  description = "The ID of the created pipeline"
  value       = grepr_pipeline.example.id
}

output "pipeline_version" {
  description = "The current version of the pipeline"
  value       = grepr_pipeline.example.version
}

output "pipeline_state" {
  description = "The current state of the pipeline"
  value       = grepr_pipeline.example.state
}

output "pipeline_health" {
  description = "The health status of the pipeline"
  value       = grepr_pipeline.example.pipeline_health
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `job_graph_json` (String) The job graph as a JSON string. Use `jsonencode()` to convert a Terraform object to JSON.
- `name` (String) The name of the pipeline. Must match the pattern `[a-z0-9_]{1,128}`. Changing this will adopt an existing pipeline with that name or create a new one.

### Optional

- `desired_state` (String) The desired state of the pipeline. Valid values are `RUNNING` or `STOPPED`. Defaults to `RUNNING`.
- `rollback_enabled` (Boolean) Whether to enable automatic rollback on update failures. Defaults to `false`.
- `state_timeout` (Number) Timeout in seconds for waiting for state transitions. Defaults to `600` (10 minutes).
- `tags` (Map of String) Custom tags for the pipeline.
- `team_ids` (Set of String) Set of team IDs that this pipeline is associated with.
- `wait_for_state` (Boolean) Whether to wait for the pipeline to reach the desired state after create/update operations. Defaults to `true`.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) The timestamp when the pipeline was created.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the pipeline (TSID format).
- `organization_id` (String) The organization ID that owns this pipeline.
- `pipeline_health` (String) The health status of the pipeline. One of `HEALTHY`, `STABILIZING`, `UNHEALTHY`, or `UNKNOWN`.
- `pipeline_message` (String) A human-readable message about the pipeline's current status.
- `state` (String) The actual current state of the pipeline.
- `updated_at` (String) The timestamp when the pipeline was last updated.
- `version` (Number) The current version of the pipeline. Increments on updates.
